Christopher Veau 09/08/2022 1.0 EX54 CR01 Pull iLPN enhancement and slight changes to the original logic Christopher Veau 10/26/2022 2.0 Complete logic change Christopher Veau 1 1 / 4/2022 2.1 Addition of Rainbow task indicator( section 6.5) Christopher Veau 11/28/2022 2.2 Made changes to when W ORK_RELEASE_BATCH_NUMBER is updated Christopher Veau 06/22/2022 2.3 Changes to INT1 /INT9 Rainbow pallet replenishment Fill pick transactions Christopher Veau 11/14/2023 2.4 Small changes to specify a rainbow task a more than one OPEN task detail (< deleted) Cody Ramangkoun 10/1/2024 2.5 Updates to include Cutdown Replenishments (INT3) into EX54 RF_FillActive Logic EX54 – Leave Different Quantity for Replens & Radial Putaway Remaining McLane generates replenishments against demand which create s INT1 and INT9 replenishments. Operationally when McLane pulls from their reserve locations for INT9 replenishments they pull a full pallet and bring the pallet to the Pick location , even for partial pallet pull tasks . McLane then replenish es the demanded quantity and either puts the remaining quantity in a nearby location , or if the user sees the entire pallet will fit in the pick location, they will leave the whole pallet at the location . For INT1 the users know what quantity can fit in the active location and will Pull the appropriate quantity from the reserve location, regardless of the task qty . This leads to pallets being put back to the reserve location with a couple cases and/ or inventory discrepancies . Additionally , scenarios may arise where McLane will have more than one replenishment task for the same item out of the same reserve location , where the user will end up bringing the reserve pallet to the pick location more than once. Lastly, McLane sometimes runs into scenarios where the user is unable to putaway all the inventory that the replenishment asks for due to inventory syncs and/or product cubing inconsistencies. For non-rainbow INT9s, it is assumed that a full pallet is pulled and WM suppresses the qty and subsequent iLPN prompt from the pull transaction. McLane then requires that users have the option to choose to enter the quantity that they actually putaway to the location. Either increasing or decreasing the replenishment allocated quantity . Base WM does not allow users to putaway a different quantity than allocated . For INT 1s, McLane requires that users have the option to choose to enter the quantity they are wanting to Pull from the reserve location. Base allows the user to enter a quantity less than or equal to the allocated quantity . WM is enhanced to give the users an option to enter a pull quantity greater than allocated as well . This is also true for rainbow-pallet INT9s (multi-detail) . With the enhancement, WM adjust s the allocated quantity and current task in accordance with the q uantity that the user enters . Additionally, WM assess the open replenishment allocations for the item when an adjustment is made and will decrement or remove allocations for the same item depending on the increase or decrease in quantity the user has putaway . WM will also create any replenishment tasks needed to fulfill any allocated quantity that was not putaway . Finally, f or INT9s WM direct s the user to putaway any remaining quantity on the pallet to a suggested location found using the existing putaway logic. An indicator is also added to the multi detailed partial pallet pull tasks to show that they are Rainbow tasks Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF Y RF_FillCasePick , RF Pu ll iLPN Reporting N Other Y INT1 and INT9 allocations and respective tasks There are nine six main types of replenishment that this enhancement applies to. F ull pallet and partial pallet replenishment s of case pick locations from reserve (INT9s) , as well as full pallet and partial pallet replenishment s of active locations from reserve (INT1s) , and full pallet and partial pallet of Case pick to active replenishment (INT3) . Rainbow pallets for both INT1 , INT3, and INT9 are also in scope for this enhancement. Case pick to active replenishment (INT3) and a A ny replenishments of cigarette items is not in scope for this modification . The RF_FillCasePick transactions is enhanced for non-rainbow partial pallet pulls to prompt the user for a putaway quantity before confirming the case pick location. Additionally for full pallet pulls user s wanting to putaway a quantity different than the allocated quantity can use the Ctrl-N key to be able to enter specific quantity . If the user enters a quantity that is equivalent to the allocated quantity, then WM will follow base update. If user enters a quantity which is different than that of the allocated quantity WM splits off the quantity from the original iLPN and then once the user scans the case pick location, puts away the new iLPN to the location. If a user enters a quantity less than the allocated quantity then WM create s a new replenishment transaction and allocation for the previously allocated quantity that was not put away. If the user enters a quantity greater than the allocated qty then WM looks to see if there are any existing replenishment tasks to decrease the quantity for. A fter the putaway , if there is still some quantity remaining on the pallet, then WM leverages the reserve putaway logic to find the best reserve location to put the items back into the reserve location . WM also initiates the User directed putaway transaction , which is enhanced to display the location found from the reserve putaway logic . This allow s the user to choose which reserve location to go to and levera ges the base User directed putaway updates to update any existing/created replenishment allocations for that LPN. T he RF Pull iLPN transaction is also enhanced so that when a user is carrying out an INT1 or rainbo w pallet INT9 and is wanting to pull a quantity different than the allocated quantity they will be able to enter a quantity greater than allocated at the base qty prompt screen. If the user enters a quantity that is equivalent to the allocated quantity, then WM will follow base update. If user enters a quantity which is different than that of the allocated quantity WM adjusts the allocated qty on the task . If a user enters a quantity less than the allocated quantity, then WM creates a new replenishment transaction and allocation for the previously allocated quantity that was not pulled. If the user enters a quantity greater than the allocated qty then WM looks to see if there are any existing replenishment tasks to decrease the quantity for. Assumptions Modification only applies to INT1 and INT9 Replenishments and does not apply to non replen pulls and putaway s No changes to replenishment task creation are in scope for this extension. Rainbow Pallets ( replenishment tasks with multiple details ) are in scope for this extension INT9 Rainbow replenishment tasks have a unique task type ( normally Replen CP – Rainbow ) INT1 Rainbow replenishment tasks have a unique task type Fill Tr ansactions associated to the R ainbow task types have parameter 3-3 Item Verification mode set to “Item is displayed, user key qty” ( linked in Task Action UI ) Fill Transactions associated to non rainbow INT9 tasks have parameter 3-3 Item Verification mode set to “ No Item verification required ” (linked in Task Action UI) The following base Inventory Need Types are the only types in scope for the modification: INT 1 – Replenish Active from Reserve INT 3 – Replenish Active from Case Pick – only changes to the fill portion INT 9 – Replenish Case Pick from Reserve Cigarette items are not in scope for this modification. No changes to the base process by which overallocation (rounding up to the UOM) is impacted by this extension. The base configuration to round up or not is still supported. No changes to the combination of P40/P50 replenishments are impacted by this extension. Th e P40/P50 combination per base allows combining of replenishments is still supported. Note, McLane controls this through pick location level configuration settings. The picking wave parameter is always left unchecked for this parameter. Follow Picking Wave Parameters are assumed static on the McLane wave templates: Active Replenishment Origin – None Note: for McLane this is configured on the item or warehouse level Combine 40/50 Replenishments – Unchecked Note: for McLane this may be checked on the pick location level Bump Up Existing 60/70 Replenishment – Checked Delay Pre-Pick Replenishment – Unchecked Item Substitution - None Perform Reserve Balancing – Unchecked Suppress Post-Pick Replenishment – ‘Select one’ For INT1 and INT9 Full Pallet Pulls users are grabbing a full pallet from reserve location For INT9 non-rainbow Partial Pallet Pulls i f the Pull transaction portion has EX54 enabled then it is assumed that users are grabbing the full pallet from reserve location and bringing said pallet to the pick location There are no INT1 single sell replenishments that are cut downs Users are completing the putaway process to allow for replenishment to allocate and tasks to be created when users confirm a fill pick quantity < QTY_ALLOC Ctrl-W and Ctrl-X disabled on Custom User Directed Putaway No changes to EX29 custom logic or behavior When carrying out Rainbow replenishments each details is put onto a separate LPN . INT3 Cutdown replenishments pull portion has custom logic supported by EX05 for partial/full pallet pulls. INT3 Cutdown replenishments Fill portion has custom logic supported by EX54 CR06 partial/full pallet pulls. Dependencies EX58 is installed and configured. EX05 is installed and configured Interface Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies N/A P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Modification logic NON -Rainbow INT9 Replenishment Flow INT1 Flow and IN T 9 Rainbow Pallet Flow Process Steps WMOS evaluates if the replenishment task is an INT1 or INT9 or is a multi-detailed replenishment . If INT1 or multi detailed INT9 follow the INT1 Flow and IN9 Rainbow Pallet Flow If INT9 and non multi detailed follow the NON-Rainbow INT9 Replenishment Flow NON-Rainbow INT9 Replenishment Flow Full pallet flow User initiates the task and scans the iLPN , user brought to location prompt for putaway If user decides to put a different qty they will use Ctrl-N to being up the qty prompt, if not they will scan the valid location and the iLPN will be putaway If Ctrl-N was used then user scans a qty , user is shown CUST 0541 Message – “Leaving different qty in location” if they scan a qty less than pallet or a qty of 0 User accept s or exit s this message is brought to location prompt for putaway User then scans valid location and is directed to User Directed putaway User scans a location to put away the remaining qty Partial Pallet Flow User initiates the task and scans the iLPN , user brought to location prompt for putaway User scans a qty and is shown CUST 0541 Message – “Leaving different qty in location” if they scan a qty different than that of the allocated qty User accepts or exits the message and is brought to location prompt for putaway User then scans valid location and is directed to User Directed putaway is there is remain in g qty in the original LPN User scans a location to put away the remaining qty INT1 Flow and IN9 Rainbow Pallet Flow User initiates the task and scans the iLPN , if Full pallet pull then user will be directed to drop location, if Partial then user is brought to a qty prompt User enters a qty at the prompt ( have the ability to enter a qty greater, less, or equal to the allocated qty) and is directed to iLPN prompt User scans a blind iLPN If single detail task user will be prompted for a drop location , if multi detailed then user will go through steps 2 and 3 repeatedly until no more details remain. NO change to process on the putaway transaction. INT 3 Fill Flow and I NT3 Rainbow Pallet F ill F low User initiates the task and scans the iLPN , user brought to location prompt for putaway User scans location for putaway User enters a qty at the prompt ( have the ability to enter a qty greater, less, or equal to the allocated qty) and is directed to iLPN prompt If single detail task user will be prompted back to the RF, if multi detailed then user will go through steps 2 and 3 repeatedly until no more details remain. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF_ Pull iLPN The RF Pull iLPN transaction is enhanced for INT1 Partial Pallet Replenishment Tasks , allowing the user to enter a quantity greater than the allocated quantity, but only up to the scanned iLPNs quantity . If a user enters a quantity greater than the allocated quantity, then WM also looks to adjust any other existing partial replenishments for that same item and destination combination. The transaction is also enhanced for INT9 Partial Pallet Replenishment Tasks . For non-rainbow pallets (only one detail on the task) WM work s under the assumption that a user is pulling a full pallet from the reserve location even for partial pallet replenishments . WM suppresses the base quantity prompt and subsequent iLPN prompt . WM handles the pull as if it were a full pallet pull, however the allocated qty will be that of the current task. Once a user has scanned the iLPN they are shown a custom Quantity prompt screen where they enter in the quantity they wish to put away to the displayed pick location. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX54 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Last , the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF_ Pull iLPN transaction parameter byte 504-504. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Validations First, WM validates that the replenishment task is an INT 1 or INT 9 Partial Pallet Replenishment task, if the replenishment task is a INT3 or a Full Pallet replenishment task then exit the EX54 custom logic for the pull transaction. Next, WM validates that non e of task details are cigarette item s , if an item is a Cigarette item, then WM exit s custom logic. Last , once the user scans the iLPN , WM does the base validations that would normally occur at the iLPN confirmation prompt. Once the validations take place the user is directed to the base quantity prompt screen. Updates Once WM has validated the above factors the following logic is used to carry out the appropriate updates. INT1 Logic The below logic only applies to for INT1 Partial Pallet replen ishment pull tasks ( TA S K_DTL.FULL_CNTR_ALLOCD = ‘ N ’ ) The below logic applies INT1 tasks that are both single detail and multi detail. For the multi detailed tasks this logic is carried out for each detail. IF QTY ENTERED = QTY_ALLOC THEN Base behavior is followed ELSE IF QTY ENTERED < QTY_ALLOC THEN Base behavior is followed ELSE IF QTY ENTERED > QTY_ALLOC THEN IF QTY ENTERED > LPN_DTL.SIZE_VALUE Throw custom message 0542 - Quantity Exceeds [ iLPN ] Quantity of [LPN_DTL.SIZE_VALUE] Bring the user back to the quantity prompt screen ELSE Make the appropriate updates to the existing allocation record Increase the quantity on the current replenishment allocation record to match the QTY ENTERED Make the appropriate updates to the current Replen Task Update the TASK_DTL.QTY_ALLOC Make the appropriate updates to the destination location’s To Be Filled qty Make the appropriate updates to the iLPN and its details The iLPN allocated qty should be the same as the iLPN qty Update the TSK_DTL.WORK_RELEASE_BATCH_NUMBER to ‘ EX54 ’ , indicating that a quantity different than the allocated qty was pulled via EX54 . McLane uses this to determine which locations the users are using this functionality often for. Check to see if there are any other partial pallet replenishment allocations for the same item going to the same destination (does not have to be for the same iLPN ) I F there are existing allocations THEN Decrement the quantity of the allocation record for partial pallet pulls by the Qty Of Difference = QTY ENTERED - QTY_ALLOC A djust the task detail(s) associated to the allocations accordingly. If more than one allocation exists for the same iLPN , decrement the quantities in order of the allocations with the smallest quantity to the largest quantity. Keep reducing allocations until the qty reduced is equal to the Qty Of Difference, or there are no more allocations to be decremented. Cancel out any tasks whose allocations have been completely removed. ELSE No action is required ELSE IF QTY ENTERED = 0 Display the base Error message “Qty Can’t Equal 0” Bring the user back to the quantity prompt screen INT9 Logic The below logic only applies to for INT 9 Partial Pallet replen ishment pull tasks ( TA S K_DTL.FULL_CNTR_ALLOCD = ‘ N ’ ) After users have scanned a valid iLPN WM carries out the below logic IF the task has more than 1 detail (Rainbow Pallet task) THEN For each detail IF QTY ENTERED = QTY_ALLOC THEN Base behavior is followed ELSE IF QTY ENTERED < QTY_ALLOC THEN Base behavior is followed ELSE IF QTY ENTERED > QTY_ALLOC THEN IF QTY ENTERED > LPN_DTL.SIZE_VALUE Throw custom message 0542 - Quantity Exceeds [ iLPN ] Quantity of [LPN_DTL.SIZE_VALUE] Bring the user back to the quantity prompt screen ELSE Make the appropriate updates to the existing allocation record Increase the quantity on the current replenishment allocation record to match the QTY ENTERED Make the appropriate updates to the current Replen Task Update the TASK_DTL.QTY_ALLOC Update the TSK_DTL.WORK_RELEASE_BATCH_NUMBER to ‘ EX54 ’ , indicating that a quantity different than the allocated qty was pulled via EX54 . McLane uses this to determine which locations the users are using this functionality often for. Check to see if there are any other partial pallet replenishment allocations for the same item going to the same destination (does not have to be for the same iLPN ) I F there are existing allocations THEN Decrement the quantity of the allocation record for partial pallet pulls by the Qty Of Difference = QTY ENTERED - QTY_ALLOC A djust the task detail(s) associated to the allocations accordingly. If more than one allocation exists for the same iLPN , decrement the quantities in order of the allocations with the smallest quantity to the largest quantity. Keep reducing allocations until the qty reduced is equal to the Qty Of Difference, or there are no more allocations to be decremented. Cancel out any tasks whose allocations have been completely removed. ELSE No action is required ELSE IF QTY ENTERED = 0 Display the base Error message “Qty Can’t Equal 0” Bring the user back to the quantity prompt screen ELSE IF Task has only 1 Detail THEN It is assumed that the user is pull ing the full iLPN qty from the reserve location WM suppresses the base quantity prompt and the subsequent base iLPN prompt. The iLPN pulled for the transaction is the initial iLPN scanned that is in the reserve location . The iLPNs should be in Allocated and Pulled status and the allocated qty should be that of the current replen task being carried out. The Pull transaction portion of the replenishment task is completed Once the user scans the iLPN the user is brought to the Custom INT9 Q uantity prompt screen, described in section 6. 3 Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base behavior for Activity Tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to PIX behavior as part of this extension LM Hooks No changes to base behavior for labor management as part of this extension. RF_FillCasePick (non – rainbow pallets) The RF _FillCasePick is enhanced to prompt the user for a quantity whenever the user is carrying out an INT9 partial pallet replenishment task or user has decide d , by using Ctrl-N , that they are wanting to put a different quantity away when carrying out a Full Pallet Replenishment task. The transaction is also enhanced to allow the user the ability to putaway the newly created iLPN if a new iLPN was created as part of the custom quantity prompt. Additionally, the transaction is enhanced to direct the users to putaway the original iLPN that was pulled from the reserve location if there is still inventory left on the iLPN . Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX54 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Last, the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF_FillCasePick transaction parameter byte 504-504. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Validations During the pull portion of the replenishment, once a user scans the iLPN , WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX54 logic. If any of the enablement features above are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base replenishment processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. NEXT, WMOS validates that the replenishment task is a INT9 task and is for an item that is NOT a cigarette item. If the replenishment is a INT3 (Cut down) then exit custom logic. If the replenishment is a INT9 replenishment but the item is a Cigarette item exit custom logic. NEXT, WMOS validates that the replenishment task has only OPEN detail ( NON-RAINBOW pallet). IF the task has more than one OPEN detail then the Rainbow pallet custom logic is carried out (section 6.3) . Next, at the base Location confirmation prompt if users choose to use the CTRL-N functionality then WM validates that the user is carrying out a Full Pallet Replenishment task. Once validated, they are brought to the custom quantity prompt defined in section 6.3. If the user is not carrying out a Full Pallet replenishment and attempts to use Ctrl-N, no thing will happen. Lastly , WM does the base validations that would normally occur at the location confirmation prompt once the user has scanned the location. RF_FillCasePick ( R ainbow pallets) The RF _FillCasePick is enhanced to allow the retention of the drop location on the original iLPN for INT9 rainbow pallet replenishment task when users decide to fill the case pick location with less than what is prompted. The base parameter 3-3 Item Verification mode set to “Item is displayed, user key qty” on the fill transaction and is leveraged to prompt the user for the q uantity . Whenever a user enters a qty less than what is prompted base deallocates the remaining quantity from the iLPN and removes the iLPN from the location. WM is enhanced to suppress the base logic of removing the iLPN from the location. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX54 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Third , the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF_FillCasePick transaction parameter byte 504-504. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Forth, the transaction specific Item Verification mode must be set pr operly . This is controlled through RF_FillCasePick transaction parameter byte 3-3. This parameter is configured as a dropdown . The option must be set to “Item is displayed, user key qty” and WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if another option is configured , this section specific control for EX54 is turned off, and below processing will not occur. Last, the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF_FillCasePick transaction parameter byte 5 2 2 -5 2 2 . This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, th is section specific control for EX54 is turned off, and below processing will not occur. Validations WMOS first carried out the base iLPN validations. NEXT, WMOS validates that the replenishment task associated to the iLPN is a INT9 task and is for an item that is NOT a cigarette item. If the replenishment is a INT3 (Cut down) then exit custom logic. If the replenishment is a INT9 replenishment but the item is a Cigarette item exit custom logic. NEXT , WMOS validates that the replenishment task has more than one OPEN detail. IF the task has only one OPEN detail , then the custom logic for non - rainbow pallets is carried out (section 6.2) . THEN , WM does the base validations that would normally occur at the location confirmation prompt once the user has scanned the location. LASTLY, WMOS carries out the base quantity validation on the quantity prompt. Updates Quantity prompt updates IF QTY ENTERED = prompted_qty THEN Base flow and updates are followed ELSE IF QTY ENTERED > prompted_qty THEN Base error message is deployed and the base flow is followed ELSE IF QTY ENTERED < prompted_qty THEN Base task detail updates are carried out Base task header updates are suppressed Create a new task detail for the remaining quantity The new task detail is tied to the original allocation of the LPN The new task detail is generated in stat code = ‘40’ Base allocation updates suppressed Let Base iLPN quantity, volume and weight updates happen. Base LPN removal logic is suppressed W M inventory record updates t he iLPN’s allocated quantity and To Be Filled quantity with the remaining quantity (this is assuming there are no other open replenishment/ allocations for that location) The iLPN’s WM inventory record updates the location_ id with the previous LPN’s location ID The iLPN will have the correct quantity and will remain in an “Allocated and Pulled” status (64) as well as remain in the staging location. Christopher Veau 09/08/2022 1.0 EX54 CR01 Pull iLPN enhancement and slight changes to the original logic Christopher Veau 10/26/2022 2.0 Complete logic change Christopher Veau 1 1 / 4/2022 2.1 Addition of Rainbow task indicator( section 6.5) Christopher Veau 11/28/2022 2.2 Made changes to when W ORK_RELEASE_BATCH_NUMBER is updated Christopher Veau 06/22/2022 2.3 Changes to INT1 /INT9 Rainbow pallet replenishment Fill pick transactions Christopher Veau 11/14/2023 2.4 Small changes to specify a rainbow task a more than one OPEN task detail (< deleted) Cody Ramangkoun 10/1/2024 2.5 Updates to include Cutdown Replenishments (INT3) into EX54 RF_FillActive Logic EX54 – Leave Different Quantity for Replens & Radial Putaway Remaining McLane generates replenishments against demand which create s INT1 and INT9 replenishments. Operationally when McLane pulls from their reserve locations for INT9 replenishments they pull a full pallet and bring the pallet to the Pick location , even for partial pallet pull tasks . McLane then replenish es the demanded quantity and either puts the remaining quantity in a nearby location , or if the user sees the entire pallet will fit in the pick location, they will leave the whole pallet at the location . For INT1 the users know what quantity can fit in the active location and will Pull the appropriate quantity from the reserve location, regardless of the task qty . This leads to pallets being put back to the reserve location with a couple cases and/ or inventory discrepancies . Additionally , scenarios may arise where McLane will have more than one replenishment task for the same item out of the same reserve location , where the user will end up bringing the reserve pallet to the pick location more than once. Lastly, McLane sometimes runs into scenarios where the user is unable to putaway all the inventory that the replenishment asks for due to inventory syncs and/or product cubing inconsistencies. For non-rainbow INT9s, it is assumed that a full pallet is pulled and WM suppresses the qty and subsequent iLPN prompt from the pull transaction. McLane then requires that users have the option to choose to enter the quantity that they actually putaway to the location. Either increasing or decreasing the replenishment allocated quantity . Base WM does not allow users to putaway a different quantity than allocated .

[Document: MCLN EX54 Leave Different Quantity for Replens & Radial Putaway Remaining]

rom the pull transaction. McLane then requires that users have the option to choose to enter the quantity that they actually putaway to the location. Either increasing or decreasing the replenishment allocated quantity . Base WM does not allow users to putaway a different quantity than allocated .

For INT 1s, McLane requires that users have the option to choose to enter the quantity they are wanting to Pull from the reserve location. Base allows the user to enter a quantity less than or equal to the allocated quantity . WM is enhanced to give the users an option to enter a pull quantity greater than allocated as well . This is also true for rainbow-pallet INT9s (multi-detail) . With the enhancement, WM adjust s the allocated quantity and current task in accordance with the q uantity that the user enters . Additionally, WM assess the open replenishment allocations for the item when an adjustment is made and will decrement or remove allocations for the same item depending on the increase or decrease in quantity the user has putaway . WM will also create any replenishment tasks needed to fulfill any allocated quantity that was not putaway . Finally, f or INT9s WM direct s the user to putaway any remaining quantity on the pallet to a suggested location found using the existing putaway logic. e pick location. Additionally for full pallet pulls user s wanting to putaway a quantity different than the allocated quantity can use the Ctrl-N key to be able to enter specific quantity . If the user enters a quantity that is equivalent to the allocated quantity, then WM will follow base update.

If user enters a quantity which is different than that of the allocated quantity WM splits off the quantity from the original iLPN and then once the user scans the case pick location, puts away the new iLPN to the location. If a user enters a quantity less than the allocated quantity then WM create s a new replenishment transaction and allocation for the previously allocated quantity that was not put away. If a user enters a quantity less than the allocated quantity, then WM creates a new replenishment transaction and allocation for the previously allocated quantity that was not pulled. If the user enters a quantity greater than the allocated qty then WM looks to see if there are any existing replenishment tasks to decrease the quantity for. UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE No MHE changes in scope for this extension. RF RF_ Pull iLPN The RF Pull iLPN transaction is enhanced for INT1 Partial Pallet Replenishment Tasks , allowing the user to enter a quantity greater than the allocated quantity, but only up to the scanned iLPNs quantity . If a user enters a quantity greater than the allocated quantity, then WM also looks to adjust any other existing partial replenishments for that same item and destination combination. The transaction is also enhanced for INT9 Partial Pallet Replenishment Tasks . For non-rainbow pallets (only one detail on the task) WM work s under the assumption that a user is pulling a full pallet from the reserve location even for partial pallet replenishments . WM suppresses the base quantity prompt and subsequent iLPN prompt . WM handles the pull as if it were a full pallet pull, however the allocated qty will be that of the current task. Once a user has scanned the iLPN they are shown a custom Quantity prompt screen where they enter in the quantity they wish to put away to the displayed pick location. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX54 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag.

[Document: MCLN EX54 Leave Different Quantity for Replens & Radial Putaway Remaining]

erwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX54 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag.

If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Last , the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF_ Pull iLPN transaction parameter byte 504-504. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Validations First, WM validates that the replenishment task is an INT 1 or INT 9 Partial Pallet Replenishment task, if the replenishment task is a INT3 or a Full Pallet replenishment task then exit the EX54 custom logic for the pull transaction. Next, WM validates that non e of task details are cigarette item s , if an item is a Cigarette item, then WM exit s custom logic. Last , once the user scans the iLPN , WM does the base validations that would normally occur at the iLPN confirmation prompt. Once the validations take place the user is directed to the base quantity prompt screen. RF_FillCasePick (non – rainbow pallets) The RF _FillCasePick is enhanced to prompt the user for a quantity whenever the user is carrying out an INT9 partial pallet replenishment task or user has decide d , by using Ctrl-N , that they are wanting to put a different quantity away when carrying out a Full Pallet Replenishment task. The transaction is also enhanced to allow the user the ability to putaway the newly created iLPN if a new iLPN was created as part of the custom quantity prompt. Additionally, the transaction is enhanced to direct the users to putaway the original iLPN that was pulled from the reserve location if there is still inventory left on the iLPN . Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX54 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Last, the transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF_FillCasePick transaction parameter byte 504-504. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below.

Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Validations During the pull portion of the replenishment, once a user scans the iLPN , WMOS validates that the warehouse is appropriately configured to enable the EX54 logic. If any of the enablement features above are not properly set, WMOS exits custom logic and continues with base replenishment processing. Otherwise, WMOS continues to the next validation. NEXT, WMOS validates that the replenishment task is a INT9 task and is for an item that is NOT a cigarette item. If the replenishment is a INT3 (Cut down) then exit custom logic. If the replenishment is a INT9 replenishment but the item is a Cigarette item exit custom logic. NEXT, WMOS validates that the replenishment task has only OPEN detail ( NON-RAINBOW pallet). IF the task has more than one OPEN detail then the Rainbow pallet custom logic is carried out (section 6.3) . Next, at the base Location confirmation prompt if users choose to use the CTRL-N functionality then WM validates that the user is carrying out a Full Pallet Replenishment task. Once validated, they are brought to the custom quantity prompt defined in section 6.3. If the user is not carrying out a Full Pallet replenishment and attempts to use Ctrl-N, no thing will happen. Lastly , WM does the base validations that would normally occur at the location confirmation prompt once the user has scanned the location. RF_ FillActive The RF _ FillActive is enhanced to allow the retention of the drop location on the original iLPN for INT1 and INT3 replenishment task when users decide to fill the active pick location with less than what is prompted. The base parameter 3-3 Item Verification mode set to “Item is displayed, user key qty” on the fill transaction and is leveraged to prompt the user for the quantity. Whenever a user enters a qty less than what is prompted base deallocates the remaining quantity from the iLPN and removes the iLPN from the location. WM is enhanced to suppress the base logic of removing the iLPN from the location. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code id EX54 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX54 is turned off, and no custom EX54 processing will occur. Third, the EX54 transaction specific flag must be enabled. This is controlled through RF_ FillActive transaction parameter byte 504-504. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below.

The remaining cases are to be brought to inventory control. Similarly if the user receives the custom message 0542 - Quantity Exceeds [ iLPN ] Quantity of [LPN_DTL.SIZE_VALUE] user is to enter the Quantity on the message and bring the extra cases ( if any) to inventory control .
